source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English Error Correction Bank
title: CAE Use of English Part 5: Error Correction (Proofreading)

sections.title: Proofreading Practice 1 - The History of Chocolate
sections.instructions: In most lines of the following text, there is one unnecessary word. It is either grammatically incorrect or does not fit the sense of the text. For each numbered line, find the incorrect word and write it in the space. Some lines are correct. If a line is correct, write 'CORRECT'.
sections.passageHtml:
The History of Chocolate

0 Chocolate is one of the most popular sweet treats in the entire world, but
00 its origins go back thousands of years to Mesoamerica. The ancient Maya
1 were the first to cultivate the cacao tree, which they believed it was
2 a gift from the gods. They prepared up a bitter, frothy drink from the beans,
3 which was often flavored with chili peppers and vanilla. For the Maya,
4 cacao beans were more valuable that they were used as a form of currency.
5 When the Spanish explorers arrived in the Americas, they were introduced
6 to this exotic beverage. Although they initially found the taste much too bitter,
7 they brought it back to Spain, where they added sugar to make it more
8 palatable to European tastes. For more than a century, the recipe was kept
9 a closely guarded secret, known only to the Spanish royalty and nobility.
10 Eventually, chocolate is spread to other European countries, where it became
11 extremely popular. In the 19th century, the industrial revolution enabled
12 the mass production of solid chocolate, which did became affordable to all.
13 Today, chocolate is a multi-billion dollar industry that it continues to
14 bring joy to millions of people, regardless of their own age or background.
15 It is also believed to have health benefits, such as the improving mood.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the extra word in capital letters, or write 'CORRECT' if the line is correct.

questions.explanation: Từ dư thừa là 'IT'. Trong mệnh đề quan hệ 'which they believed was...', đại từ quan hệ 'which' đóng vai trò làm chủ ngữ bổ nghĩa cho 'the cacao tree'. Việc thêm 'it' vào sau 'they believed' tạo ra sự lặp chủ ngữ dư thừa. Method: complete the gap with "IT" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the extra word in capital letters, or write 'CORRECT' if the line is correct. Line 1:. Correct completion: IT.

acceptedAnswers: Từ dư thừa là 'UP'. Động từ 'prepare' chuẩn bị
questions.explanation: Từ dư thừa là 'UP'. Động từ 'prepare' (chuẩn bị/pha chế) đi kèm trực tiếp với tân ngữ 'a bitter drink', không sử dụng phrasal verb 'prepare up' trong ngữ cảnh này. Method: complete the gap with "UP" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.

acceptedAnswers: which did became affordable to all
questions.explanation: Từ dư thừa là 'DID'. Mệnh đề quan hệ 'which became affordable' đã có động từ chính chia ở quá khứ đơn là 'became'. Trợ động từ 'did' đứng trước 'became' là dư thừa và sai ngữ pháp (nếu muốn nhấn mạnh thì phải dùng 'did become', nhưng ở đây không cần thiết và cấu trúc 'did became' là lỗi double-past). Method: complete the gap with "DID" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write the extra word in capital letters, or write 'CORRECT' if the line is correct. Line 12:. Correct completion: DID.

sections.title: Proofreading Practice 2 - The Invention of the Internet
sections.instructions: In most lines of the following text, there is one unnecessary word. It is either grammatically incorrect or does not fit the sense of the text. For each numbered line, find the incorrect word and write it in the space. Some lines are correct. If a line is correct, write 'CORRECT'.
sections.passageHtml:
The Invention of the Internet

0 The internet has revolutionized the way we live, work, and communicate, but
00 its development was a gradual process that spanned several decades.
1 It began in the late 1960s as a research project funded by the US
2 Department of Defense, which it wanted to create a communication network
3 that could survive from a military attack. This early network, ARPANET,
4 connected computers at several universities and research institutions.
5 Over the next two decades, scientists developed the protocols that they would
6 allow different computer networks to communicate with each other.
7 In 1989, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web, which it made
8 the internet accessible to the general public. He designed the first
9 web browser and web server, and created the HTML language for to format
10 web pages. By the mid-1990s, the internet was beginning to be grow rapidly,
11 with millions of users online. Today, the internet is an more essential part
12 of daily life, connecting billions of people and devices around the globe.
13 It has transformed industries, enabled instant communication, and made
14 information accessible to anyone with an internet connection. However, that it
15 also poses challenges, such as cyber threats and the digital addiction.
